articles.headline: Video: PR Indonesia advances the jamu industry into modern wellness business
articles.summary: BBPOM remains reliant on imports despite the national jamu industry's potential reaching Rp350 trillion per year. Ultra Sakti CEO Harun Pramono said only 71 of 20,000 licensed jamu products can be upgraded to Standardized Herbal Medicine due to strict CPOTB standards. The company is working to educate SMEs and expand its market.
articles.keywords: BBPOM, jamu, CPOTB, Ultra Sakti, standardized herbal

articles.headline: BPOM Finds 22 Male Stamina and Pain‑Relief Drugs Dangerous, Can Cause Stroke and Kidney Damage
articles.summary: BPOM in Jakarta disclosed 22 hazardous natural‑ingredient drug products, including 13 male stamina items, of which 10 have a Distribution Permit Number. The products contain sildenafil, tadalafil, dexamethasone and other substances that can trigger stroke, kidney damage and heart disorders. BPOM pledged to take firm action against the perpetrators.
articles.keywords: BPOM,stamina drugs,hazardous chemicals,stroke,kidney

articles.headline: BBPOM Pekanbaru Partners with Several Agencies to Safeguard Riau from Certain Drug Hazards
articles.summary: On 20 May 2026, BBPOM Pekanbaru held a national OOT prevention action at Balai Pauh Janggi, the Governor’s residence in Riau, attended by 24 representatives from BBPOM, BNNP, the Health Department and other agencies. The event concluded with a joint commitment to tighten monitoring and education on the misuse of Tramadol, Triheksifenidil, Ketamin and Dextromethorphan in Riau.
articles.keywords: BBPOM, OOT, Tramadol, Riau, collaboration

articles.headline: BPOM wins National Archiving Award 2026 with AA rating
articles.summary: BPOM received the 2026 National Archiving Award in the Ministry/Agency Cluster II category with an AA “Very Satisfactory” rating. The award was presented by ANRI during a coordination meeting in Jakarta on 20 May 2026, as motivation for accountable archive management.
articles.keywords: BPOM, National Archiving Award, AA rating, Taruna Ikrar, Jakarta

articles.headline: BPOM Bali Seizes 173,000 Dangerous Drug Tablets
articles.summary: BPOM Denpasar seized 173,000 unregistered drug tablets worth about Rp200 million, including trihexyphenidyl, tramadol and ketamine, after uncovering a fraudulent livestock vitamin B Complex scheme. Acting head BPOM Made Ery Bahari reported the arrest of 15 suspects over three years, focusing on shipments via courier services in Denpasar and Badung.
articles.keywords: BPOM, dangerous drugs, Denpasar, Trihexyphenidyl, misuse

articles.headline: BBPOM seizes 173,000 dangerous drug tablets in Bali
articles.summary: BBPOM Denpasar seized 173,000 tablets of unregistered drugs worth Rp200 million, including triheksifenidil, tramadol and ketamine, which were distributed via courier services in Denpasar and Badung. Acting Head of BBPOM Made Ery Bahari said 15 perpetrators have been arrested over the past three years.
articles.keywords: BBPOM,unregistered drugs,Denpasar,triheksifenidil,tramadol

articles.headline: 173,000 dangerous drug tablets seized in Bali to prevent misuse
articles.summary: The BBPOM office in Denpasar seized 173,000 dangerous drug tablets worth Rp200 million, including trihexyphenidyl, tramadol and ketamine, after uncovering a scheme that disguised them as livestock vitamins. Acting Head Made Ery Bahari said 15 suspects were arrested and that the agency is cooperating with police and a shipping company to prevent misuse.
articles.keywords: BBPOM, dangerous drugs, Trihexyphenidyl, Denpasar, misuse

articles.headline: 173,000 dangerous drug tablets seized in Bali to curb misuse
articles.summary: BBPOM Denpasar seized 173,000 dangerous OOT drug tablets worth Rp200 million, including Triheksifenidil, Tramadol and Ketamine, in Denpasar and Badung. Fifteen suspects were arrested in cooperation with police, BNN and a courier company, as part of the national OOT misuse prevention campaign.
articles.keywords: BBPOM,dangerous drugs,OOT,Bali,Triheksifenidil

articles.headline: Momentum of National Jamu Day 2026, BPOM Head in Sofifi Educates on Dangers of Jamu Containing Pharmaceutical Chemicals
articles.summary: North Maluku BPOM head Ermanto Siahaan appeared on the Tribun Guest podcast in Sofifi on 20 May 2026, warning the public about the danger of jamu containing pharmaceutical chemicals (BKO). He stressed the importance of consuming jamu that is labeled or authorized by BPOM to avoid organ damage such as to the kidneys, liver, and stomach.
articles.keywords: BPOM, jamu, BKO, North Maluku, Sofifi

articles.headline: BBPOM Samarinda holds education to prevent misuse of certain drugs
articles.summary: BBPOM Samarinda, led by Agung Kurniawan, organized the 'National Action for Prevention of Misuse of Certain Drugs (OOT)' education on Tuesday at the KPPN Samarinda hall, emphasizing cross‑sector collaboration to protect youth from illegal hard drugs. Speakers included the Director of Narcotics Research of Polda Kaltim.
articles.keywords: BBPOM,Samarinda,OOT,education,drug misuse

articles.headline: BPJS Kesehatan Kebumen Branch ensures SPPG volunteers are protected by JKN
articles.summary: BPJS Kesehatan Kebumen Branch, led by Head Dina Diana Permata, ensured that SPPG volunteers in Kebumen Regency are enrolled in JKN to support the Free Nutritious Meal Program. The outreach emphasized health protection for volunteers so they can perform duties without cost concerns, and the local government pledged to monitor and adjust participation.
articles.keywords: BPJS Kebumen, SPPG, JKN, Free Nutritious Meal Program, volunteers

articles.headline: Combat Illegal Drugs, BPOM Samarinda Opens Complaint Center and Partners with BNN Tanah Merah
articles.summary: BPOM Samarinda held a national action to prevent misuse of illegal drugs (OOT) on 19 May 2026 at the KPPN Samarinda auditorium, East Kalimantan, together with the KPPN head, Dirresnarkoba of Polda Kaltim, and the head of BNN Tanah Merah. The event opened a complaint center and urged the public to report OOT such as tramadol, ketamine and dextromethorphan via BPOM Samarinda, Balikpapan, or BNN.
articles.keywords: BPOM, Samarinda, OOT, BNN Tanah Merah, complaint center

articles.headline: Papua Barat provincial government integrates mobile health services in Manokwari
articles.summary: The mobile health service integration program in Manokwari was carried out from 15–20 May 2026 in Kampung Syou, Amber, and Aserbey, Mokwam District. The West Papua Health Office (Dinkes Papua Barat) combined family herbal plant empowerment and acupressure, involving pediatric, ENT, and other specialists, in accordance with Permenkes No 9/2016.
articles.keywords: Papua Barat,Manokwari,mobile health services,family herbal plants,acupressure

articles.headline: Komisi VII DPR urges government to optimise UMKM to tackle global shock
articles.summary: Chair of Komisi VII DPR Saleh Partaonan Daulay urged the Ministry of UMKM to strengthen the sector’s role in confronting the global economic shock. He highlighted the need for comprehensive data on UMKM participants, including recipients of Kredit Usaha Rakyat, as the One Data Indonesia Bill is being drafted.
articles.keywords: Komisi VII DPR,UMKM,Kredit Usaha Rakyat,One Data Indonesia Bill,Saleh Partaonan Daulay

articles.headline: BPOM Finds Hazardous Cosmetics, UMY Lecturer Warns of Liver Damage Risk up to Cancer
articles.summary: BPOM identified 11 cosmetic products containing the red dye K10 (Rhodamin B) and other hazardous substances such as mercury, hydroquinone, retinoic acid, dexamethasone, and 1,4-dioxane. The findings were published on the Universitas Muhammadiyah Yogyakarta website on 15 May 2026. These substances can cause skin irritation, liver dysfunction, and cancer with continuous use.
articles.keywords: BPOM, cosmetics, Rhodamin B, mercury, hydroquinone

articles.headline: Cek KLIK Revived, BPOM Sanggau Warns of Dangers of Illegal and Expired Products
articles.summary: BPOM Sanggau held a Public Consultation Forum on 13 May 2026 in the ballroom of The Garden Palace Hotel, Sanggau City, West Kalimantan, to receive public complaints about food products. BPOM head Erik Budianto Tampubolon stressed the importance of the Cek KLIK principle (packaging, label, marketing authorization, expiration) and pledged to trace stale food findings still on the market.
articles.keywords: BPOM, Cek KLIK, Public Consultation Forum, food products, Sanggau

articles.headline: On Menkes Budi Gunadi’s Ir title, ITB explains
articles.summary: ITB explained that before 1993 diplomas did not list titles, so the use of the Ir title by alumni, including Menkes Budi Gunadi Sadikin, is traditional. The Engineer title only gained a legal basis through Law No.11/2014 and the 1993 regulation.
articles.keywords: Menkes Budi Gunadi,ITB,Ir title,Law No.11/2014,1993

articles.headline: DPR RI asks government to strengthen peak Hajj mitigation, focusing on Armuzna crowd density and pilgrim safety
articles.summary: Deputy Chair of Komisi VIII DPR RI, Singgih Januratmoko, urged the government to strengthen 2026 Hajj mitigation at Arafah, Muzdalifah and Mina, tackling crowd density, extreme weather and health services for pilgrims over 50. Transport, cooling tents and medical aid have been readied, with >80% arriving in good condition.
articles.keywords: DPR, Hajj mitigation, Armuzna, pilgrim safety, Kementerian Haji

articles.headline: Life‑protecting virus, phage therapy amid antibiotic resistance
articles.summary: A WHO and Kemenkes RI survey found that 22.1% of the population used oral antibiotics in a year, 41% without a prescription, and an estimated 133,800 deaths were linked to AMR in 2019. The government launched the National Antimicrobial Resistance Control Strategy 2025‑2029, while phage therapy is being considered as a precision alternative for resistant bacterial infections.
articles.keywords: antibiotic resistance, AMR, phage therapy, WHO, Kementerian Kesehatan

articles.headline: Note, BPOM Withdraws 11 Hazardous Cosmetics from Market, Popular Anti‑Dandruff Shampoos Also Highlighted
articles.summary: BPOM identified 11 cosmetic products containing hazardous substances such as mercury, hydroquinone, and 1,4‑dioxane, and revoked their market authorisation. Two popular anti‑dandruff shampoos, SELSUN 7 Herbal and SELSUN 7 Flowers, were withdrawn because their 1,4‑dioxane levels exceeded the limit. BPOM head Taruna Ikrar said the findings resulted from routine surveillance across Indonesia.
articles.keywords: BPOM, hazardous cosmetics, mercury, SELSUN, 1,4-dioxane
